evals.prompt: I'm building an AI code review platform called CodeLens AI. It analyzes pull requests, detects bugs, and suggests improvements using machine learning. I need a landing page that looks like something from Awwwards — dark theme, futuristic feel, with a 3D hero section that has floating geometric shapes or maybe a glowing AI brain/sphere. Include a features section with 4 capabilities (Bug Detection, Code Suggestions, Security Scanning, Performance Analysis), a 'How It Works' section with 3 steps, social proof logos, pricing with 3 tiers, and a final CTA. Use Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind, Three.js with React Three Fiber, Framer Motion for scroll animations. Make it premium.
evals.expected_output: A complete Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind project with a dark-themed landing page featuring a Three.js hero scene (floating geometry or AI sphere with bloom post-processing), glass navbar, social proof section, 4 feature cards with scroll-reveal animations, 3-step how-it-works section, glassmorphism pricing cards (3 tiers), testimonials, final CTA with ambient 3D background, and responsive footer. All sections use Framer Motion scroll animations. Mobile fallback for 3D. Production-ready component architecture.
expectations: Uses Next.js App Router with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
Dark theme with cohesive color palette (indigo/purple accent system)
Three.js / React Three Fiber hero scene with floating 3D elements
3D scene wrapped in Suspense with loading fallback
Bloom or post-processing on 3D elements for cinematic glow
Hero section has display-size typography (48px+) with gradient text
Features section shows 4 capabilities with icons and scroll-reveal animation
How It Works section with 3 numbered steps
Social proof section with company logos
Pricing section with 3 tiers, one visually highlighted as recommended
Glassmorphism styling on cards (backdrop-blur, translucent backgrounds)
Framer Motion scroll animations (fade + slide-in reveals, stagger on grids)
Responsive design with mobile breakpoints (hamburger nav, stacked cards)
3D fallback for mobile devices (CSS animation or static visual)
Component-based architecture with separate files for sections and 3D scenes
Consistent spacing scale and typography hierarchy throughout

evals.prompt: Build me a portfolio website for a creative studio called 'Nebula Creative'. They do branding, web design, and motion graphics. I want it to feel like an interactive art experience — bold typography, warm color palette (copper/gold accents on dark background), and a 3D hero with morphing organic shapes or a blob that reacts to the mouse cursor. The site should have: a dramatic hero with the studio name in huge type, a project showcase gallery with hover effects, an about section, a services breakdown (3 services), client testimonials, and a contact section. Use scroll-driven GSAP animations — text reveals, parallax layers. Next.js + Tailwind + Three.js. Make it feel like an award-winning creative agency site.
evals.expected_output: A premium creative studio portfolio using Next.js + Tailwind with warm dark theme (copper/gold accents). Features a Three.js hero with morphing blob or organic shape responding to mouse, GSAP scroll-driven text reveals and parallax, project gallery grid with hover effects, services section, testimonials, and contact form. Bold display typography with mixed weights. Feels like a top-tier creative agency website.
expectations: Uses Next.js with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
Dark background with warm color palette (copper, gold, terracotta accents)
Three.js hero scene with morphing organic shape (MeshDistortMaterial or custom shader)
Mouse-reactive 3D element (follows cursor or distorts on hover)
GSAP ScrollTrigger animations — text character reveals, parallax depth
Bold display typography (64px+) with a mix of weights and possibly italic accents
Project gallery with grid layout and interactive hover effects (scale, overlay reveal)
Services section showing 3 distinct services with descriptions
Client testimonials section with realistic placeholder content
Contact section with form or contact information
Scroll-driven animations feel choreographed — proper stagger and timing
Responsive design with mobile fallback for 3D and simplified animations
Generous spacing between sections (80px+ on desktop)
The overall design feels creative, bold, and agency-caliber — not template-like

evals.prompt: I need a landing page for a cybersecurity product called ShieldNet. It provides real-time threat detection and network monitoring for enterprise companies. The hero should have a 3D globe showing network connections or attack vectors with glowing cyan dots and connection arcs. Dark theme with cyan/teal accents. Sections: hero with headline 'Real-Time Threat Intelligence, Global Scale', trusted-by enterprise logos (Microsoft, Google, AWS, IBM, Cisco), 3 key features (Threat Detection, Network Monitoring, Incident Response) with icons, a live stats section showing numbers like '99.9% Uptime' and '2M+ Threats Blocked', a how-it-works section, pricing for Enterprise and Pro plans, and a strong CTA. Use React Three Fiber for the globe, Framer Motion for UI animations. Next.js + Tailwind.
evals.expected_output: A cybersecurity-themed Next.js landing page with dark theme and cyan accents. Features a 3D globe built with React Three Fiber showing connection points and arcs, enterprise-grade social proof, 3 feature cards, animated stat counters, how-it-works section, 2-tier enterprise pricing, and final CTA. The design conveys trust, security, and technical sophistication.
expectations: Uses Next.js with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
Dark theme with cyan/teal accent color system
3D globe visualization built with React Three Fiber
Globe has visible dots/nodes and connection arcs or lines
Globe rotates slowly with ambient animation
Hero headline matches or closely follows the requested text
Enterprise logo bar with at least 5 company names/logos
3 feature cards (Threat Detection, Network Monitoring, Incident Response)
Animated stat counters (numbers that count up on scroll)
How-it-works section with numbered steps
Pricing section with at least 2 tiers
Framer Motion scroll-reveal animations on sections
Responsive design — globe has mobile fallback
Typography hierarchy is clear with proper heading sizes
The design conveys enterprise-grade security and trust
